channel.title: Markets News Today
channel.description: Breaking news and expert analysis on business, finance, technology and world affairs.

item.title: Steelers Blindsided: Eagles Swooped Makai Lemon Before Pick
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers believed wide receiver Makai Lemon was theirs for the taking in the 2026 NFL Draft, only to discover the Philadelphia Eagles had already snatched him off the board. The chaotic sequence unfolded during a compressed selection window that left Pittsburgh scrambling to adjust on the fly. It was one of the most dramatic behind-the-scenes moments of draft weekend.

item.title: Mike Vrabel's Hubris Made Him the 2026 NFL Draft's Main Character
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NFL Draft was widely panned as lacking energy and star power, but New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel found a way to inject himself into the national conversation. A tense interaction with a prominent NFL reporter and the resurgence of a simmering sex scandal turned Vrabel into the most talked-about figure of draft weekend.

item.title: US Eases Marijuana Restrictions in Major Win for Cannabis Industry
item.description: The United States Justice Department has officially reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous controlled substance, marking one of the most significant shifts in federal drug policy in decades. The landmark decision is expected to unlock new financial opportunities for the legal cannabis industry, which has struggled under the weight of federal restrictions and banking barriers. Industry analysts say the move could trigger a wave of investment and consolidation across the sector.

item.title: Missing Scientists Spark Conspiracy Theories, Hurt Families
item.description: A growing number of deaths and disappearances among individuals connected to sensitive United States research programs have ignited fierce speculation online. While conspiracy theories spread rapidly across social media platforms, the families of those affected are pleading for compassion and truth. Experts warn that misinformation surrounding these cases is causing measurable harm to grieving loved ones.

item.title: Trump Eyes Taxpayer Takeover of Spirit Airlines at Right Price
item.description: President Donald Trump confirmed Thursday that his administration is actively considering a taxpayer-funded acquisition of Spirit Airlines, the embattled budget carrier that filed for bankruptcy protection in late 2024. Trump indicated the government would purchase the airline at the right price with the strategic intent of reselling it once oil prices fall. The proposal has already sparked significant debate among economists, aviation industry experts, and lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Extended 3 Weeks After Trump Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Sunday that the fragile Israel-Lebanon ceasefire has been extended by three weeks following a second round of intensive peace talks at the White House. The breakthrough comes amid mounting international pressure to prevent a renewed escalation of hostilities between the two nations. Diplomats on both sides described the extension as a critical window of opportunity to forge a more durable long-term agreement.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for US-Iran Peace Talks, Condemns Death Penalty
item.description: Pope Leo XIV issued an urgent appeal Saturday for the United States and Iran to return to the negotiating table, calling for a peaceful resolution to the ongoing conflict between the two nations. Speaking aboard the papal plane returning from a historic trip to Africa, the pontiff also took a firm stance against capital punishment, calling it an affront to human dignity.

item.title: U.S. Soldier Accused of $400K Gain on Maduro Capture Bet
item.description: A U.S. Army soldier is under investigation for allegedly using classified intelligence to place lucrative bets on the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, netting approximately $400,000 through online prediction markets. The case has ignited a fierce debate about the intersection of national security and the rapidly expanding prediction market industry. Federal investigators are now examining whether the soldier exploited privileged military knowledge to gain an unfair advantage on platforms that allow users to wager on real-world geopolitical events.

item.title: Seahawks Select RB Jadarian Price With Final First-Round Pick
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks closed out the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft by selecting running back Jadarian Price with the final pick of the opening night. General Manager John Schneider had openly pursued a trade-down opportunity throughout the evening but ultimately could not find a willing partner before the clock ran out.

item.title: Matlock Boss Breaks Down Finale's Wellbrexa Wrap-Up
item.description: After two seasons of courtroom intrigue, Matlock finally delivered its long-awaited verdict in the Wellbrexa pharmaceutical case during a gripping Season 2 finale. The showrunner is now opening up about how that pivotal storyline wrapped up and what fans can expect from a dramatically reimagined Season 3, including a surprising time jump.

item.title: Eagles Earn Top Draft Grades for 2026 NFL First-Round Pick
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles walked away from the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft with their reputation as one of the league's shrewdest front offices firmly intact. General manager Howie Roseman and his staff drew widespread praise from analysts and draft experts for their selection. The pick is being viewed as a potential cornerstone addition to an already talented roster hungry for another championship run.

item.title: DOJ Watchdog Probes Epstein Act Compliance Failures
item.description: The Justice Department's internal watchdog has launched a formal investigation into whether federal agencies have fully complied with the Epstein Files Transparency Act, a landmark law requiring the release of documents related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The probe raises serious questions about transparency obligations and potential obstruction within federal bureaucracy. Officials and advocates are demanding answers about what records may still be withheld from the public.

item.title: Meet the 19-Metre Octopus That May Have Ruled Ancient Seas
item.description: New fossil evidence points to the existence of truly colossal octopuses that may have stretched up to 19 metres in length, prowling the ancient oceans more than 100 million years ago. Scientists believe these giants could have been among the most dominant predators of the Cretaceous seas. The discovery is reshaping our understanding of how complex and diverse marine life was long before modern creatures evolved.

item.title: US Reclassifies State-Licensed Marijuana as Less Dangerous Drug
item.description: The United States Justice Department has officially reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous controlled substance, marking one of the most significant federal drug policy shifts in decades. The decision is expected to dramatically reshape the legal cannabis industry, which has faced persistent financial and regulatory headwinds despite widespread state-level legalization. Industry analysts say the move could unlock billions of dollars in economic activity and bring long-sought relief to cannabis businesses struggling under outdated federal restrictions.

item.title: 1 Dead, 9 Injured in Shooting at Mall of Louisiana
item.description: A violent shooting erupted at the Mall of Louisiana on Friday, April 24, 2026, leaving one person dead and nine others wounded in what authorities are calling a devastating incident. Two of the injured victims remain in critical condition as investigators work to piece together the events that led to the attack. Law enforcement flooded the scene as terrified shoppers scrambled for safety inside one of Baton Rouge's most popular retail destinations.

item.title: Xbox's New Chief Asha Sharma Reevaluates Exclusive Games Strategy
item.description: Microsoft's newly appointed Xbox chief Asha Sharma is shaking up the gaming division's long-standing approach to exclusive titles, signaling a significant strategic pivot for the brand. Sharma, who took the helm of Xbox earlier this year, has confirmed she is actively reevaluating which games should remain platform-exclusive and which should reach broader audiences. The move could fundamentally reshape the competitive landscape of console gaming as Microsoft looks to reestablish Xbox as a dominant force in the industry.

item.title: Chinese Satellites Over Mideast Raise Alarms for U.S. Forces
item.description: China's rapidly expanding constellation of surveillance satellites passing over the Middle East has put American military planners on high alert, raising fears that U.S. forces and allied operations could be monitored in near real time. Defense officials warn that the intelligence advantage once held exclusively by Western powers is eroding at an accelerating pace. The development represents one of the most significant shifts in battlefield transparency in a generation.

item.title: Meta to Cut 8,000 Jobs as AI Investment Surge Reshapes Tech Giant
item.description: Meta Platforms has announced it will cut approximately 8,000 jobs in what will become the company's largest round of layoffs since 2023. The move comes as the social media and technology conglomerate dramatically increases spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and development. Employees had reportedly been bracing for the cuts for several weeks ahead of the official announcement.

item.title: Judge Orders Release of Family Held Longest in ICE Detention
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the release of a mother and her five children who became the longest-held family in immigration detention in the United States. Hayam El Gamal and her children endured more than 10 months at a facility in Dilley, Texas, amid a legal battle drawing national attention. The ruling marks a significant moment in the ongoing debate over family immigration detention policies.

item.title: Bucks Finalizing Deal to Hire Taylor Jenkins as Head Coach
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks are closing in on a significant leadership change, finalizing a deal to bring Taylor Jenkins aboard as the franchise's new head coach. Jenkins, who previously helmed the Memphis Grizzlies, represents a bold hire as Milwaukee looks to reset its competitive direction. The move signals a new chapter for a Bucks organization hungry to return to championship contention.

item.title: Intel Stock Soars After Q1 Earnings Beat and Strong Q2 Outlook
item.description: Intel delivered a powerful first-quarter earnings report that surpassed Wall Street expectations on both revenue and profit, sending shares sharply higher in after-hours trading. The chipmaker also issued a stronger-than-anticipated outlook for the second quarter, fueling optimism that its long-awaited turnaround is finally gaining traction. Investors and analysts are now reassessing Intel's competitive position in the global semiconductor landscape.

item.title: Senate Passes Budget Resolution After Six-Hour Vote-A-Rama
item.description: The United States Senate adopted a sweeping budget resolution on Friday following an exhausting six-hour "vote-a-rama" session that stretched into the early morning hours. Republicans successfully advanced their fiscal framework without a single Democratic vote, clearing a critical path toward funding immigration enforcement operations. The resolution marks a major step forward in the GOP's legislative agenda for 2026.

item.title: Iran Raises Hormuz Stakes by Seizing Vessels Mid-Conflict
item.description: Iran has dramatically escalated its strategy in the ongoing conflict by seizing commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway through which nearly 20 percent of the world's oil supply passes. The ship captures, which began weeks after Tehran blocked the strait on February 28, have sent shockwaves through global energy markets and drawn sharp condemnation from Western governments. Analysts warn that Tehran is using maritime aggression as a calculated bargaining chip in negotiations it insists will only begin after foreign forces withdraw from the region.

item.title: Middle East Ceasefires Under Strain as Navy Secretary Ousted
item.description: Two fragile ceasefire agreements in the Middle East are showing serious signs of strain, raising fears of renewed conflict across the region. Simultaneously, a significant leadership shake-up within the U.S. military establishment has removed the Secretary of the Navy from his post, adding further uncertainty to American defense posture at a critical moment.

item.title: Gilgo Beach Killer's Ex-Wife Still Sleeps in the Kill Room
item.description: The former wife of convicted Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann has made a haunting revelation: she continues to live in the Long Island home where her husband murdered multiple women, sleeping each night in the very room where the killings took place. In a candid and deeply unsettling documentary interview, she describes her nightly torment as she relives the final moments of his victims from within those walls.

item.title: Celeste Rivas Hernandez Cause of Death Revealed in D4vd Case
item.description: Authorities have officially disclosed the cause of death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose case has shocked the music world and broader public alike. Rising R&B and indie pop singer D4vd, born David Anthony Burke, stands charged with first-degree murder in connection with her death. The case has brought intense scrutiny to the 21-year-old artist, who rose to fame at a remarkably young age.

item.title: Trump Administration Reclassifies Cannabis as Less Dangerous Drug
item.description: The Trump administration has officially reclassified cannabis from a Schedule I to a Schedule III controlled substance, marking one of the most significant shifts in American drug policy in decades. The move is intended to accelerate medical research and expand patient access to marijuana-based treatments. Health advocates and lawmakers across the political spectrum are calling the decision a historic turning point in how the United States approaches cannabis regulation.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Pull Back From Record Highs
item.description: U.S. stocks declined Thursday as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite pulled back from the record highs they achieved just one session earlier. Investors appeared to take profits following a powerful rally that had pushed both indexes to historic territory. The retreat came amid ongoing scrutiny of corporate earnings, interest rate expectations, and broader macroeconomic signals.

item.title: Vrabel to Seek Counseling After Russini Incident Fallout
item.description: New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel made a stunning personal announcement Wednesday evening, revealing he plans to seek professional counseling in the wake of what he described as a "completely innocent interaction" with veteran NFL reporter Dianna Russini. The statement came during Vrabel's second press conference of the week and left reporters, fans, and league observers with more questions than answers. The development adds yet another unexpected chapter to what has already been a turbulent offseason for the Patriots franchise.

item.title: Warner Bros Shareholders Back Paramount Merger, Reject CEO Pay
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders voted on April 24, 2026 to approve the highly anticipated merger with Paramount Skydance, marking a seismic shift in the media landscape. However, the vote came with a pointed message as shareholders simultaneously rejected the executive compensation packages for CEO David Zaslav and other top company brass. The dual outcome signals investor support for consolidation while expressing growing frustration over executive pay at a time of continued financial turbulence in the streaming wars.

item.title: Tesla Stock Falls on Massive Spending Plans and Musk Remarks
item.description: Tesla shares dropped sharply Friday after the electric vehicle giant disclosed plans to dramatically accelerate capital spending to over $25 billion in 2026, a figure that stunned Wall Street analysts. The announcement, combined with candid and at times unsettling remarks from CEO Elon Musk during the company's earnings call, sent the stock tumbling as investors weighed the risks of such an aggressive expansion strategy. The selloff added to a turbulent year for Tesla, which has faced mounting pressure on multiple fronts.

item.title: Rotavirus Surge Hits U.S. Hard, Certain Groups at Risk
item.description: A highly contagious rotavirus outbreak is spreading rapidly across the United States, with Centers for Disease Control and Prevention data confirming positive test rates have surpassed last year's peak levels. Health officials are urging parents, caregivers, and vulnerable populations to take immediate precautions. Certain patient groups face dramatically heightened risks as the stomach bug continues its aggressive seasonal spread.

item.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Shareholders Vote on Paramount Acquisition
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery is holding a pivotal special shareholder meeting on Thursday to vote on Paramount's proposed acquisition of the company. The deal, if approved, could fundamentally reshape the global media and entertainment industry. Investors and industry analysts are watching closely as the outcome may determine the future of two of Hollywood's most iconic studios.

item.title: Trump Reclassifies Medical Marijuana in Historic Policy Shift
item.description: In a landmark move that advocates have pursued for decades, the Trump administration has officially reclassified state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous substance under federal law. The order, signed by the acting attorney general, marks one of the most significant shifts in American drug policy in generations. The decision separates medical cannabis from Schedule I status, where it had been grouped alongside heroin since the early 1970s.

item.title: 2026 NFL Mock Draft: Final Round 1 Predictions Before Tonight
item.description: The 2026 NFL Draft is finally here, and Round 1 kicks off tonight with more uncertainty at the top than any draft in recent memory. Quarterback decisions, surprise trade rumors, and defensive studs all collide in a first round that promises fireworks from pick one through pick thirty-two. Here is everything you need to know before the selections are announced.

item.title: Senate Launches Vote-a-Rama to Fund ICE Without Democrats
item.description: The Senate entered a grueling marathon voting session Wednesday as Republicans advanced a plan to fund immigration enforcement agencies within the Department of Homeland Security, bypassing Democratic cooperation entirely. The so-called vote-a-rama is expected to stretch for hours, with Democrats prepared to force votes on dozens of amendments. The move marks one of the sharpest partisan showdowns in the chamber this year over immigration policy and federal funding priorities.

item.title: DWTS Season 35 Cast: Maura Higgins & Ciara Miller Revealed
item.description: Dancing With the Stars has officially kicked off its Season 35 casting announcements by revealing two exciting new celebrity competitors. Reality television personalities Maura Higgins and Ciara Miller are the first names confirmed for the upcoming season, setting the stage for what promises to be a thrilling competition on the ballroom floor.

item.title: Pistons Dominate Magic 98-83 in Commanding Victory
item.description: The Detroit Pistons put together one of their most complete performances of the season, defeating the Orlando Magic 98-83 on April 22, 2026. Detroit's defense was suffocating throughout the contest, holding Orlando to well below their season scoring average. The victory marks a significant statement win for a Pistons team that has been building momentum at a critical point in the season.

item.title: Flyers Crush Penguins 5-2, Take 3-0 Series Lead in Playoffs
item.description: The Philadelphia Flyers delivered a thunderous 5-2 victory over the Pittsburgh Penguins on Friday night, seizing a 3-0 series advantage and reminding the NHL that this city is back. Wells Fargo Center was electric from the opening puck drop as Philly fans welcomed playoff hockey home with deafening intensity. This is not a fluke, and the Flyers are proving it one dominant performance at a time.

item.title: UK Parliament Passes Historic Bill to Ban Future Cigarette Sales
item.description: The United Kingdom has taken a landmark step in public health policy, passing legislation that will permanently ban cigarette sales to anyone born after December 31, 2008. The Tobacco and Vapes Bill, approved by Parliament on Tuesday, represents one of the most sweeping anti-smoking measures ever enacted in a major democracy. The bill now awaits formal royal assent before becoming law.

item.title: 'Storage Wars' Star Darrell Sheets Dead at 67
item.description: Darrell Sheets, the charismatic fan favorite known as "The Gambler" on A&E's hit reality series "Storage Wars," has died at the age of 67. Sheets passed away Wednesday in Lake Havasu City, Arizona, leaving behind a legacy that helped define reality television's golden era. His memorable presence across 163 episodes made him one of the most recognizable faces in unscripted television history.

item.title: RFK Jr. Senate Hearing Exposes Cassidy's Competing Loyalties
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. faced pointed questions from a Senate panel this week, but no exchange drew more attention than the one involving Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana. The Republican senator, who wears the hats of legislator, medical doctor, and reelection hopeful, demonstrated just how difficult it can be to navigate political loyalty, professional conscience, and electoral survival all at once.

item.title: Spirit Airlines Nears $500 Million Federal Bailout Deal
item.description: Spirit Airlines is reportedly on the verge of receiving a $500 million lifeline from the federal government as the struggling ultra-low-cost carrier fights for survival. The potential bailout, backed by the Trump administration, would represent one of the most significant government interventions in the airline industry in recent years. Sources familiar with the negotiations say a deal could be finalized in the coming weeks.

item.title: Michigan State Lands 7-2 Transfer Center Anton Bonke
item.description: Michigan State basketball has secured one of the most intriguing big men in the transfer portal, landing 7-foot-2 center Anton Bonke from Charlotte. Bonke averaged 10.6 points and a team-high 8.3 rebounds last season, bringing a rare combination of size, skill, and an unusual personal background to East Lansing.

item.title: Chemical Leak at West Virginia Plant Kills 2, Injures 19
item.description: A catastrophic chemical leak at a West Virginia industrial facility has claimed two lives and hospitalized 19 others, including one person in critical condition. Emergency crews responded Wednesday to the Catalyst Refining facility in Kanawha County as hazmat teams worked to contain the dangerous situation. Authorities are investigating the cause of the incident while surrounding communities remain on alert.

item.title: Logan Airport Measles Exposure Alert Issued for Travelers
item.description: Massachusetts public health officials have issued an urgent alert warning that travelers passing through Boston Logan International Airport may have been exposed to measles after a confirmed infected passenger entered the terminal. Measles is among the most contagious infectious diseases known to medicine, capable of spreading through the air long after an infected person has left a space. Anyone who was present during the identified exposure window is urged to check their vaccination status immediately and contact a healthcare provider.

item.title: Tesla Q1 Revenue Rises on EV Sales and FSD Subscriptions
item.description: Tesla posted a notable revenue rebound in the first quarter of 2026, buoyed by recovering electric vehicle deliveries and a surge in Full Self-Driving subscription revenue. The results mark a meaningful improvement over the troubled first quarter of 2025, even as the company continues to pour capital into ambitious long-term bets including robotics, artificial intelligence, and its own semiconductor fabrication facility. Investors responded cautiously but with optimism as Tesla reaffirmed its vision for a technology-driven future beyond the automobile.

item.title: Gene Therapy for Inherited Deafness Shows Lasting Results
item.description: A major multicentre clinical trial has demonstrated that gene therapy for OTOF-related deafness is safe, well-tolerated, and capable of restoring meaningful hearing across a broad age range. Published in Nature, the findings tracked 42 participants for up to 2.5 years, offering the most comprehensive long-term evidence yet for this pioneering treatment. The results are being hailed as a transformative moment in the treatment of hereditary hearing loss.

item.title: Navy Secretary Ousted Amid Ongoing US Naval Blockade of Iran
item.description: Navy Secretary John Phelan has been abruptly removed from his position, six sources familiar with the matter confirmed, as the United States maintains a high-stakes naval blockade of Iran. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell announced Wednesday evening that Phelan would be departing effective immediately, sending shockwaves through the defense community. The sudden leadership change raises serious questions about civilian oversight of the Navy at a moment of unprecedented military tension in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Alan Osmond, Eldest of The Osmonds, Dies at Age 76
item.description: Alan Osmond, the eldest member of the iconic family singing group The Osmonds, has passed away at the age of 76. His family remembered him as the quiet backbone of one of America's most beloved musical dynasties. His death marks the end of an era for a group that defined wholesome pop entertainment for millions around the world.
